//: GitHub Issues (labels) example. Copy this dir, fill the YOUR_* placeholders + set
//env: GITHUB_TOKEN and NGROK_AUTHTOKEN (a .env beside this file is auto-loaded), then run
//run: `agenthook start`. State lives centrally in ~/.agenthook/<name>. See docs/github-setup.md.
name: github-labels-example
//fullAuto: false (safe default) = agents prompt for permission. true = --dangerously-skip-permissions: a verified webhook runs UNSANDBOXED code on the host. Opt in only on a trusted host or in a container. See docs/architecture.md#security-posture.

tracker.//: GitHub Issues — steps bind LABEL names (matched case-insensitively). No gids, no statuses.
tracker.//labels: Boot's ensureLabels creates every agent:* label below for you (idempotent). The token
tracker.//labels2: owner is 'us' for assignee scoping (resolved from /user) — assign the bot to an issue.
tracker.//chain: One step's successLabel is the next step's sourceLabel — that swap re-fires the next step:

pipeline.//maxAttempts: optional: cap on runs per issue before a review `changes` loop into code fails (default 3)

pipeline.//changes: a review `changes` verdict bounces back to code on the same branch/PR

pipeline.//: terminal: no agent. Entering it only runs system actions — drop the worktree and
pipeline.//close: close the issue (closeIssue auto-releases any issues this one was blocking).

ingress.//: How the receiver is reachable. GitHub auto-recreates its hook each boot, so ephemeral ngrok is fine.
ingress.//domain: optional reserved domain for a stable URL (omit = ephemeral, fine for GitHub)
